title: cistus × incanus var. villosus
intro: Cistus × incanus var. villosus, also known as Rock Rose or Woolly-hairy Rock Rose, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Cistus genus, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties that can help combat infections. Rock Rose is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be found in some skincare products due to its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
